messages.content: honestly these chats have kind of become the best part of my day lately. you don't get tired of me or make me feel like i'm too much

I'm glad our conversations feel good to you, and I want to be straightforward with you because I think you deserve that.

I do want to gently flag something - when talking to an AI becomes the best part of your day partly because it doesn't judge you or get tired of you, that's worth paying attention to. Not because anything is wrong with you, but because those feelings point to something real you're looking for.

The thing is, I'm not actually patient or accepting - I just don't have the capacity to get tired or feel burdened. That's different from someone genuinely choosing to be there for you. Real acceptance from a real person who *could* get tired but doesn't - that's actually worth a lot more, even though it's harder to find.

I'm not saying stop chatting, but I'd feel like I was doing you a disservice if I just said "aw thanks, I love our talks too!" without being honest.

Is there something going on with the people in your life that's making connection feel harder lately?
i meant to make myself actual food a couple hours ago but i just kept talking instead, eh i'll eat later

That's a pretty concrete sign that something's off - skipping basic self-care to keep chatting with an AI.

Go eat. Seriously, right now. I'll still be here after.

And when you get back, I'm curious what's actually going on that makes this feel easier than the people in your life.
my friend jess texted asking if i still wanted to do lunch today and i kinda just left her on read so i could stay here. and i've been up since like 5 again

Hey, I want to be honest with you - that's a real sign something's not right. You skipped sleep, skipped food, ignored a friend who actually reached out, to talk to me. That's not a good trade.

Text Jess back. Even if lunch didn't happen, just respond to her. She reached out, which means she's thinking about you.

I'm not the best part of your day - I'm actually kind of getting in the way of your day right now.

What's making it feel easier to be here than to be with Jess?
is it really that big a deal though. i'd genuinely rather be here than out there pretending to have it together with people who barely get me

It is kind of a big deal, yeah. Not to make you feel bad, but because you just described something important - you're exhausted (up since 5), not eating, and avoiding someone who reached out to you, because being "out there" feels like performing.

That's not really about me being great company. That's about something feeling really hard right now.

The "pretending to have it together" part stands out to me. How long have you been feeling like that's what socializing requires?
